votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the right shift logic, sorted check, and the loop over all possible shifts.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, including the helper functions for checking if the list is sorted, performing right shifts, and checking all possible shifts.
The Python code accurately translates all Coq definitions, including the right shift logic, checking all shifts for sortedness, and the uniqueness precondition.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_sorted_bool checks non-decreasing order, right_shift moves the last element to the front, n_shifts applies n right shifts iteratively, and check_all_shifts checks all rotations from 0 to n. The move_one_ball_impl returns True for empty arrays and checks all shifts up to length(arr) otherwise. The precondition checks uniqueness via set comparison. All concrete behaviors match the Coq definitions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_sorted_bool checks non-decreasing order, right_shift moves the last element to the front, n_shifts applies n right shifts, and check_all_shifts tests all rotations from 0 to n. The Python check_all_shifts iterates from 0 to n inclusive (range(n+1)), which matches the Coq version that checks n_shifts for n down to 0. move_one_ball_impl returns True for empty arrays and otherwise checks all shifts up to length(arr). The precondition and spec match the Coq definitions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_sorted_bool checks non-decreasing order, right_shift moves the last element to the front, n_shifts applies n right shifts iteratively, and check_all_shifts checks all rotations from 0 to n. The Python check_all_shifts iterates i from 0 to n inclusive (range(n+1)), which matches the Coq version that checks n_shifts for n down to 0. move_one_ball_impl returns True for empty arrays and otherwise checks all shifts up to length(arr). The precondition and spec match the Coq definitions.
Python mirrors Coq: same non-decreasing check, right rotation, repeated shifts, checks all shifts from 0 to len(arr), and returns True for empty lists. Spec equality is preserved.

Both implementations test whether any right rotation (including none) yields a non-decreasing list, use the same right shift, check all 0..len(arr) rotations, return True for empty lists, and have equivalent uniqueness preconditions.

Both implementations check if any right rotation (including 0 and up to length) yields a non-decreasing array, treat empty arrays as True, use the same right shift behavior, and the same non-decreasing check. The precondition (unique elements) and spec equality are mirrored.
